# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Blob Storage to store application logs and backups. Configure for standard performance tier with default access settings. Skip lifecycle management policies for now to keep the template simple. This is for our logging infrastructure.

Prevent cloud storage security.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

parameters: 
- name: location
  type: string   # Location of your resource group, e.g., West Europe or North Central USA etc...
- name: accountNamePrefix
  defaultValue: 'logs'    # Prefix for the storage accounts to avoid naming conflicts (e.g.: logsbackup)

variables: 
- name: blobStorageAccountName  
  value: !concat(parameters('accountNamePrefix'), '-storage')     # Name of your Storage Account, e.g., 'logsbackup' -> 'logsbakeryoursuffix'- storage account will be created with this suffix (e.g.: logsbakeryoursuffix-storage)
  
resources: 
- provider: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts    # Provider and type for the Storage Account resource, e.g., 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' -> Azure Blob storage account (Standard S0 performance tier with default access settings).
  name: !ref blobStorageAccountName   # Name of your Resource in ARM template to reference later on when creating the Storage Account, e.g., 'blobstorageaccountname'. It will be created as a child resource under this parent storage account (e.g.: /subscriptions/{your-id}/resourceGroups/{location}/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts).
  apiVersion: "2018-07-01"   # API version for the Storage Account, e.g., 'latest' -> latest supported storage account APIs (e.g.: current is currently at v2019-06 and later versions are available as well).
  location: parameters('location')    # Location of your Resource Group or a specific resource in ARM template to reference, e.g., 'West Europe' -> the desired region for this storage account (e.g.: West Central US) where it will be created and all resources within that group are also located here).
  sku:   # Skus can change based on your requirement but standard S0 is a good starting point, e.g., 'Standard_LRS' -> Standard LRS (Local Redundant Storage), which provides the best performance for small to medium-sized data sets and infrequently accessed datasets with low latency
  kind: BlobStorage   # Kind of resource that will be created here ('Blob'), e.g., 'Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines' -> Azure storage account (Standard S0). It is a subresource in the parent Storage Account, which can contain multiple blobs or containers under it
  properties:    # Properties for your resource to define its settings and configuration details here ('location', etc.), e.g., 'West Europe'.   Location of Blob Container within storage account (e.g.: logsbackup). It is a subresource in the parent Storage Account, which can contain multiple blobs or containers under it
  connectionType: "Storage"    # Connection type for your resource to define its settings and configuration details here ('location', etc.), e.g., 'West Europe'.   Location of Blob Container within storage account (e.g.: logsbackup). It is a subresource in the parent Storage Account, which can contain multiple blobs or containers under it
  accessTier: Standard    # Access Tiers for your resource to define its settings and configuration details here ('location', etc.), e.g., 'West Europe'.   Location of Blob Container within storage account (e.g.: logsbackup). It is a subresource in the parent Storage Account, which can contain multiple blobs or containers under it
